Form 2276 (01/21) Page 1 of 3
Form 2276
Subcontractor Nonconformance Report
(NCR)
Project ID or Subcontract Title (entered by subcontractor)
Revision number of this NCR
LANL subcontract technical representative (STR) upon receipt
Received By Z Number
Date Received Signature
Instructions for subcontractor
1. Subcontractors will use this form to receive Los Alamos National Laboratory (LANL) approval or rejection
of the proposed disposition and to notify LANL:
a. when a nonconformance is identified, including items that do not meet subcontract requirements,
and to document the subcontractor’s proposed action(s) together with the technical justification;
b. when provided services are not performed in accordance with subcontract requirements and to document
the subcontractor’s proposed action together with the technical justification;
c. upon failure to meet requirements of approved subcontractor submittal documents; and/or
d. upon identification of potential suspect/counterfeit items.
2. Submit completed form and supporting documentation by email to STR within four (4) calendar days after
identification or detection of nonconforming item or service, unless otherwise directed in subcontract documents.
3. Complete all applicable fields in Section 1. If the information requested does not apply to this nonconformance,
state “not applicable” or “N/A.”
4. Attach additional supporting information whenever necessary.
5. Always include required markings and follow required protections if the form contains classified or controlled
unclassified information.
6. A copy of completed form will be returned to the subcontractor after LANL evaluation and after disposition.
1.0 Subcontractor’s nonconformance from contractual requirements
1.1 Subcontractor’s NCR No. (if any)
1.2 Date Submitted
1.3 Subcontractor’s Organization Information
Organization Name
Address Point of Contact Phone Email
1.4 Subcontract No. Purchase Order No. STR’s Name
1.5 Supplier/Vendor Supplier/Vendor Part No. Item Description
1.6 Date Nonconformance Detected Detection Method
Location of Nonconformance (e.g., technical area and building number)
1.7 Previous NCR submittals for same or similar issue (if any)
1.8 Date STR Notified

[Page 4]
Form 2276 Subcontractor Nonconformance Report (NCR)
Ref. Doc: P330-6 Nonconformance Control and Reporting    Page 1 of 6 Form 2276 Instructions
Revised: [11/19/2020]
FORM 2276 INSTRUCTIONS (Remove these instructions upon submittal to LANL.)
PURPOSE
The Subcontractor N
onconformance Report (NCR) (Form 2276) is used by subcontractors to report
nonconformances to LANL and to document the extent of condition and the technical justification, approval, and
satisfactory completion of the disposition. This form also identifies LANL actions such as the evaluation and
approval of the disposition, cross-referencing of design-change and USQ documentation when required, and
NCR closure requirements.
APPLICABILITY
Form
2276 applies to subcontractor nonconformances. Form 2276 replaces:
• The subcontractor nonconformance reporting function of Form 2178 Conduct of Engineering
Subcontractor Deviation Disposition Request (SDDR). Form 2276 is not a design-change or
subcontract-modification document; subcontractors must still use Form 2178 to document,
disposition, review, and approve deviations to subcontract requirements, but not to notify
LANL of NCRs.
• Form AP-350-410-FM01, Subcontractor Nonconformance Report Form, associated with AP-350-410,
Nonconformance Reporting. Form AP-350-410-FM01 was formerly used by subcontractors t o transmit
NCRs for construction activities subject to SD350, Project Management for Capital Asset Acquisition
and Construction.
For
guidance on use of common subcontractor forms, see Table 1 below.
PRECAUTIONS AND LI
MITATIONS
1. Subcontractors with their own approved NCR programs must manage nonconformances in accordance
with their approved NCR program. However, all subcontractors (whether or not they have their own
NCR process) shall submit NCRs for LANL approval using Form 2276.
2. The subcontractor NCR is not a vehicle for granting variances from LANL institutional documents. If a
nonconformance disposition requires a variance from an institutional document, then the variance
process of the appropriate institutional document (e.g., Engineering Standards) must be followed. See
Table 1.
3. Prior to approving a nonconformance disposition, LANL will consider whether the disposition will
adversely affect important properties of functions of the item or the service.
4. NCRs and supporting documentation that contain controlled unclassified information must be marked
and controlled in accordance with P204-1, Controlled Unclassified Information. Documents that contain
classified information must be marked and controlled in accordance with P204-2, Classified Matter
Protection and Control Handbook.
GENERAL
1. When there is
no STR, STR functions relating to this form will be performed by the LANL technical point
of contact.
2. For construction projects, reference to this form is typically made in Subcontract Exhibit D and/or Exhibit
H.
3. When this form is used by organizations whose job titles are not mentioned in this document, equivalent
functions may be substituted (e.g., Design Authority for FDAR).
4. Submittal of nonconformances from lower-tier subcontractors shall be through the prime subcontractor.
SECTION 1: SUBCONTRACTOR NOTIFICATION TO LANL AND STR RECEIPT
Section Subcontractor Information
1.1 Enter the subcontractor’s document number by which the subcontractor tracks this NCR under their
own system. If none, enter N/A.
1.2 Enter the date on which the subcontractor submits the NCR to LANL.
1.3 Enter the subcontractor’s organization name, address, point of contact name, telephone number,
and POC e-mail address.

[Page 5]
Form 2276 Subcontractor Nonconformance Report (NCR)
Ref. Doc: P330-6 Nonconformance Control and Reporting            Page 2 of 6 Form 2276 Instructions
Revised: [11/19/2020]
Section Subcontractor Information
1.4 Enter LANL subcontract number, purchase order number, and STR’s name.
1.5 Enter supplier or vendor of the item, part number, and item description, if applicable. If none, enter
N/A.
1.6
Enter the date nonconformance detected. Enter the location of the nonconformance, such as the
technical area (TA) and building number.
Enter the method used to detect the nonconformance (e.g., visual inspection, testing, receipt
inspection, etc.).
1.7 List previously submitted NCRs, including dates for same or similar nonconformances, if
applicable. If none, enter N/A.
1.8 Enter the date STR was notified.
1.9
Describe the nonconforming item. Include reference to specific requirement(s) not met, including
drawing, specification, code/standard and citation, as applicable.
Include the extent of condition for each identified item, quantity affected, and key characteristics
such as serial, lot, batch, heat or other numbers as appropriate.
Attach reproducible-quality documents as necessary.
1.10
Select the proposed disposition:
1. Use-As-Is: The item, though nonconforming, is acceptable for its intended use based on an
evaluation by a subject matter expert (engineer of record/design agency when required).
2. Repair: The item must be restored to a condition such that the capability of the item to function
reliably and safely is unimpaired, even though the item does not conform to the original
requirement.  Repaired items must be reexamined in accordance with applicable procedures
and original or alternate acceptance criteria.
3. Rework: The item must be made to conform to original requirements using approved processes
or procedures. Reworked items must be reexamined in accordance with applicable procedures
and original acceptance criteria.
4. Reject: The item is unacceptable for its intended use.
The disposition is not accepted until Section 2 is completed and signed by specified LANL
personnel.
1.11
Describe the proposed disposition and an estimated date for completion. Provide technical
justification for LANL to evaluate.
Attach legible and reproducible quality copies of supporting documentation.
For proposed repairs, submit acceptance criteria to be used, including a detailed repair procedure
or a reference to a procedure previously submitted for use in similar situations.
1.12
Describe the compensatory actions that the subcontractor is proposing (when required) and actions
to ensure that nonconformances of a similar nature are prevented from recurring.
1.13
The subcontractor’s authorized representative enters their name, title, telephone number, signature
and date. Signature indicates that the NCR is complete, accurate, and ready for submittal. Submit
completed form to LANL STR.
LANL Actions
Section STR Entry
Top of
Page 1
Upon receipt, STR enters Z number and signs and dates the form at the top of page 1. Signature
indicates receipt of the NCR.
Notify the owning manager (OM) and forward the signed form and all supporting documentation to
the OM and nonconformance reporting point of contact (NCR POC). (The NCR POC is the QA
representative, if any; otherwise, the NCR coordinator.)
SECTION 2: LANL EVALUATION AND DISPOSITION
The disposition evaluator is responsible for completing sections 2.2—2.6 and for considering whether the
nonconformance will adversely affect important properties or functions of the item or the service. The disposition
evaluator is the subject matter expert (SME) assigned by the OM to evaluate a subcontractor’s proposed
disposition. The disposition evaluator must have:
• Demonstrated competence in the specific subject area they are evaluating

[Page 6]
Form 2276 Subcontractor Nonconformance Report (NCR)
Ref. Doc: P330-6 Nonconformance Control and Reporting            Page 3 of 6 Form 2276 Instructions
Revised: [11/19/2020]
• An adequate understanding of the requirements
• Access to pertinent background information
• Authority to evaluate and recommend acceptance of the subcontractor’s proposed disposition
For nonconformances subject to SD350, Project Management for Capital Asset Acquisition and Construction,
the project engineer (PE) is the disposition evaluator unless noted otherwise. Prior to approving a subcontractor
NCR, the PE must consult with the engineer of record/design agency for technical evaluation and disposition,
when applicable (as when the proposed disposition is repair or use as is).
Section  LANL Entry Information
2.0 NCR POC reviews the form for completeness and adequate description of the nonconforming
condition, proposed corrective action, appropriate disposition category, and technical justification. If
acceptable, proceed to 2.1; otherwise, return to subcontractor through STR to make any necessary
revisions.
2.1 NCR POC assigns a cause code and sends the form to the disposition evaluator. See final page of
these instructions for a table of NCR cause codes.
2.2 Disposition evaluator checks the appropriate box for management level of the affected item, if
applicable.
2.3 For nuclear facilities, disposition evaluator checks the functional classification box for the affected
item or service. For nonnuclear structures, systems or components (SSCs), check “Nonsafety” box.
Check yes or no to indicate whether a USQD/USID is required. If yes, enter the USQD/USID
number.
2.4 Disposition evaluator actions:
1. Check all applicable boxes to define LANL actions.
2. Describe any LANL actions in association with the subcontractor’s proposed disposition.
3. Check whether the nonconformance is likely to be GIDEP reportable. If yes, fill out a draft of the
GIDEP report form and submit it to the GIDEP representative.
4. If other subcontractors a re affected indicate who they are and the document that initiated
resolution of that involvement.
5. If the disposition is repair or use as is, the disposition affects the design.
a. S end the NCR form to the responsible engineer/engineer of record (RE/EOR) for
concurrence, justification, and signature in Section 2.9.
b. The RE/EOR returns the form to the disposition evaluator.
6. Review proposed corrective or compensatory actions (when required) and actions to prevent
recurrence listed in Section 1.12. If any revisions are needed, state them.
7. If FDAR approval is required, the disposition evaluator checks yes in Section 2.8 and sends the
form to the FDAR for review, concurrence, and signature in Section 2.8.
2.5 Disposition evaluator provides appropriate justification for LANL actions checked in Section 2.4.
When changes to LANL drawings, specifications, requisitions, or other LANL documents are
required, reference the documents in the table in this section.
2.6  Disposition evaluator checks whether the subcontractor’s proposed disposition is preliminarily
accepted or rejected. If rejected, state reason in Section 2.4 and return this form to the STR.
2.7 Disposition evaluator enters name, Z number, organization, signature, and date.
Project engineer signs for construction projects to signify that appropriate LANL and Design
Agency personnel have evaluated the NCR and concur with the disposition or have provided an
alternative disposition, as applicable.
If no conditional release (CR) or additional approvals are required, check no in Sections 2.8, 2.9,
and 2.10 and send a copy of the form to the STR and/or document control coordinator (DCC) for
distribution to subcontractor and project team as applicable.
2.8 When the disposition is use as is or repair within a facility or project subject to AP-341-621, Design
Authority Technical Review, the facility design authority representative (FDAR) for the accepting
organization must review and approved the disposition. The FDAR signature signifies thorough
review of safety basis, environmental safety and health, regulatory, and LANL institutional
requirements resulting from the nonconformance. When FDAR approval is required, enter name, Z
number, organization, signature, and date of review.

[Page 7]
Form 2276 Subcontractor Nonconformance Report (NCR)
Ref. Doc: P330-6 Nonconformance Control and Reporting            Page 4 of 6 Form 2276 Instructions
Revised: [11/19/2020]
Section  LANL Entry Information
When additional approval is needed, route to that person for evaluation and signature in Section
2.9. If no is checked for Section 2.9 but a CR is required, route to the NCRC or STR as applicable
for completion of Section 2.10. If no CR or other approvals are required, check no in Sections 2.9
and 2.10 and send a copy of the form to the STR and/or document control coordinator (DCC) for
distribution to subcontractor and project team as applicable.
2.9 When other approval is required the additional approver evaluates the proposed disposition and, if
approved, signs and dates Section 2.9. If no CR is required, check no in Section 2.10 and send a
copy of the form to the STR and/or document control coordinator (DCC) for distribution to
subcontractor and project team as applicable.
2.10 When a CR is needed, the STR or NCRC initiates an NCR in the NCR Tool, enters the required CR
information in the NCR Tool, and enters the NCR initiation date in Section 2.10.
Once all required sections are completed, if the NCRC has completed Section 2.10, the NCRC sends
a copy of the form to the STR and/or document control coordinator (DCC) for distribution to
subcontractor and project team as applicable.
SECTION 3: VERIFICATION OF WORK COMPLETION (REQUIRED FOR REPAIR OR REWORK ONLY)
The verifier is responsible to verify completion of work in accordance with the NCR disposition. Complete this
section for NCRs dispositioned as repair or rework. Verification is not needed for use-as-is or reject dispositions
because they do not involve additional steps beyond the use or rejection of the item.
Section  Section 3 – Verifier Entry Information
3.1 The verifier inspects completed work and supporting documentation in accordance with the NCR
disposition and confirms that the NCR can be closed. (Note: If the work has not been executed
effectively, the disposition verifier will notify the STR to work with the subcontractor to resolve any
issue(s).) Reference design-change documentation, if any. The disposition verifier marks the
applicable work verification block and enters name, Z number, organization, signature, and date.
Signature indicates that the disposition was adequately performed and documented.
Disposition verifier submits completed form and documentation to NCR POC when no additional verification is
required. When additional verification is required, the disposition verifier forwards the form to the additional
verifier.
3.2 When additional verification is required, the additional verifier enters name, Z number, organization,
signature, and date. Submit the form and documentation to NCR POC.
Section  NCR POC Entry Information
3.3 Enter name, Z number, organization, signature, and date.
Review the subcontractor NCR and documentation for evidence of closure and form completeness.
Signature indicates that these documents are complete and the NCR is ready to close. Submit the
form and supporting documentation to the STR.
Section STR Entry Information
4.1 STR enters name, Z number, organization, signature, and date. Signature indicates concurrence
that the NCR documents and actions are complete, including any actions that may affect the terms
of the subcontract, and the NCR is ready to close.
STR submits completed form to the NCR POC and appropriate document control and record management office
for final distribution and records management.

[Page 8]
Form 2276 Subcontractor Nonconformance Report (NCR)
Ref. Doc: P330-6 Nonconformance Control and Reporting            Page 5 of 6 Form 2276 Instructions
Revised: [11/19/2020]

[PDF VISUAL ENRICHMENT - OLLAMA VLM]
Model: qwen3-vl:235b-cloud
Renderer: pypdfium2
Route source: manifest
Pages described: 1, 2, 9

Route reason: Pages 1 and 9 contain large embedded visual/scanned regions; page 2 is a visually structured form/table page.


## Page 1 visual notes
### Visual extraction
- **Important visible text and headings**  
  - Top right: "Form 2276"  
  - Title: "Subcontractor Nonconformance Report (NCR)"  
  - Blue header: "Instructions for subcontractor"  
  - Blue section header: "1.0 Subcontractor's nonconformance from contractual requirements"  
  - Gray-shaded field label: "LANL subcontract technical representative (STR) upon receipt"  
  - Footer: "Form 2276 (01/21)", "Page 1 of 3"  

- **Tables/checklists/forms, with row or field meanings**  
  - Structured form with labeled input fields:  
    - "Project ID or Subcontract Title (entered by subcontractor)"  
    - "Revision number of this NCR"  
    - "Received By" / "Z Number" (side-by-side fields)  
    - "Date Received" / "Signature" (side-by-side fields)  
    - Section 1.3: "Organization Name", "Address", "Point of Contact", "Phone", "Email"  
    - Section 1.4: "Subcontract No.", "Purchase Order No.", "STR's Name"  
    - Section 1.5: "Supplier/Vendor", "Supplier/Vendor Part No.", "Item Description"  
    - Section 1.6: "Date Nonconformance Detected", "Detection Method", "Location of Nonconformance (e.g., technical area and building number)"  

- **Diagrams/charts/logos/stamps/signatures and what they indicate**  
  - Top-left logo: "Los Alamos NATIONAL LABORATORY EST.1943" with stylized atom-like icon  
  - No visible stamps or signatures (fields present but empty)  

- **Key facts useful for search and question answering**  
  - Form purpose: For subcontractors to report nonconformances and request LANL approval/rejection of proposed dispositions  
  - Submission requirement: Within four (4) calendar days after identification of nonconforming item/service  
  - Instructions section has 6 numbered items covering when to use the form, submission requirements, field completion rules  
  - Section 1.1-1.8 fields document nonconformance details including detection date, location, previous similar issues  
  - Field labeling uses specific terms: "Z Number", "STR" (Subcontract Technical Representative), "NCR" (Nonconformance Report)


## Page 2 visual notes
### Visual extraction
- **Important visible text and headings**  
  - Section labels: `1.9 Description of nonconformance...`, `1.10 Proposed disposition`, `1.11 Technical justification...`, `1.12 Compensatory actions`, `1.13 Subcontractor’s Authorized Representative`, `Section 2: LANL evaluation and approval of disposition`, `2.0 Received by NCR Point of Contact (POC)`, `2.1 Cause code assigned by NCR POC`, `To be completed by the disposition evaluator`, `2.2 Affected item or structure...`, `2.3 Affected SSC functional classification`, `2.4 LANL actions`, `2.5 LANL Disposition Statement`  
  - Notes: "If design changes (e.g., field change requests) are required, the disposition shall be considered repair (not conforming to original requirements) rather than rework."  

- **Tables/checklists/forms**  
  - `1.10 Proposed disposition`: Checkboxes for `Use-As-Is`, `Repair`, `Rework`, `Reject`  
  - `1.13 Subcontractor’s Authorized Representative`: Columns for `Name`, `Title`, `Phone`, `Signature`, `Date`  
  - `2.0 Received by NCR POC`: Columns for `Name`, `Z Number`, `Organization`, `Signature`, `Date`  
  - `2.2 Affected SSC management level`: Checkboxes for `ML-1`, `ML-2`, `ML-3`, `ML-4`, `N/A`  
  - `2.3 Affected SSC functional classification`: Checkboxes for `Safety Significant`, `Safety Class`, `Other Hazard Control`, `Defense in Depth`, `Nonsafety`  
  - `2.4 LANL actions`:  
    - `Is this GIDEP reportable?` (Yes/No)  
    - `Other subcontractors affected?` (Yes/No)  
    - Table for document changes: Columns `Document No.`, `Revision No.`, `Document Title`, `Description` (3 empty rows)  
  - `2.5 LANL Disposition Statement`: Checkboxes for `Use-As-Is`, `Repair`, `Rework`, `Reject`  

- **Diagrams/charts/logos/stamps/signatures**  
  - Form identifier: `Form 2276 (01/21)` at bottom left  
  - Page footer: `Page 2 of 3` at bottom right  
  - No visible charts, diagrams, or signatures (fields are blank)  

- **Key facts useful for search and question answering**  
  - Form number: `2276`, revision date `01/21`  
  - Disposition options: `Use-As-Is`, `Repair`, `Rework`, `Reject` (repeated in 1.10 and 2.5)  
  - Management levels: `ML-1` to `ML-4`  
  - Safety classifications: `Safety Significant`, `Safety Class`, `Nonsafety`  
  - GIDEP reportability question and USQ/USI determination (`Yes/No` fields)  
  - Document change tracking table (empty) for `drawings, specifications, requisitions`


## Page 9 visual notes
### Visual extraction
- **Headings**: Form 2276, Subcontractor Nonconformance Report (NCR), Table 2: NCR Cause Codes  
- **Table structure**:  
  - Columns: *Category* (purple header), *Apparent Cause Code*, *Definition*  
  - Categories:  
    - **MAN - WOMAN (i.e., WORKER)**  
      - Cause codes: Lack of training/qualification, Training/qualification inadequate, etc.  
    - **METHOD**  
      - Cause codes: Unintentional human error, Planning not sufficient, etc.  
    - **MACHINE**  
      - Cause codes: Equipment failure, Wrong equipment used, etc.  
- **Key metadata**:  
  - Ref. Doc: P330-6 Nonconformance Control and Reporting  
  - Page: 6 of 6 (visible at bottom)  
  - Form 2276 Instructions Revised: [11/19/2020]  
- **Visual details**:  
  - Purple column headers for "Category," "Apparent Cause Code," and "Definition"  
  - Definitions include specific failure descriptions (e.g., "Training completed by worker was not sufficient...")  
  - No signatures, charts, or logos visible.

Form 2276
Subcontractor Nonconformance Report
(NCR)

Revision number of this NCR
LANL subcontract technical representative (STR) upon receipt
Received By Z Number
Date Received Signature
Instructions for subcontractor
1. Subcontractors will use this form to receive Los Alamos National Laboratory (LANL) approval or rejection
of the proposed disposition and to notify LANL:
a. when a nonconformance is identified, including items that do not meet subcontract requirements,
and to document the subcontractor’s proposed action(s) together with the technical justification;
b. when provided services are not performed in accordance with subcontract requirements and to document
the subcontractor’s proposed action together with the technical justification;
c. upon failure to meet requirements of approved subcontractor submittal documents; and/or
d. upon identification of potential suspect/counterfeit items.
2. Submit completed form and supporting documentation by email to STR within four (4) calendar days after
identification or detection of nonconforming item or service, unless otherwise directed in subcontract documents.
3. Complete all applicable fields in Section 1. If the information requested does not apply to this nonconformance,
state “not applicable” or “N/A.”
4. Attach additional supporting information whenever necessary.
5. Always include required markings and follow required protections if the form contains classified or controlled
unclassified information.
6. A copy of completed form will be returned to the subcontractor after LANL evaluation and after disposition.

[Page 4]
Form 2276 Subcontractor Nonconformance Report (NCR)
Ref. Doc: P330-6 Nonconformance Control and Reporting    Page 1 of 6 Form 2276 Instructions
Revised: [11/19/2020]
FORM 2276 INSTRUCTIONS (Remove these instructions upon submittal to LANL.)
PURPOSE
The Subcontractor N
onconformance Report (NCR) (Form 2276) is used by subcontractors to report
nonconformances to LANL and to document the extent of condition and the technical justification, approval, and
satisfactory completion of the disposition. This form also identifies LANL actions such as the evaluation and
approval of the disposition, cross-referencing of design-change and USQ documentation when required, and
NCR closure requirements.
APPLICABILITY
Form
2276 applies to subcontractor nonconformances. Form 2276 replaces:
• The subcontractor nonconformance reporting function of Form 2178 Conduct of Engineering
Subcontractor Deviation Disposition Request (SDDR). Form 2276 is not a design-change or
subcontract-modification document; subcontractors must still use Form 2178 to document,
disposition, review, and approve deviations to subcontract requirements, but not to notify
LANL of NCRs.
• Form AP-350-410-FM01, Subcontractor Nonconformance Report Form, associated with AP-350-410,
Nonconformance Reporting. Form AP-350-410-FM01 was formerly used by subcontractors t o transmit
NCRs for construction activities subject to SD350, Project Management for Capital Asset Acquisition
and Construction.
For
guidance on use of common subcontractor forms, see Table 1 below.
PRECAUTIONS AND LI
MITATIONS
1. Subcontractors with their own approved NCR programs must manage nonconformances in accordance
with their approved NCR program. However, all subcontractors (whether or not they have their own
NCR process) shall submit NCRs for LANL approval using Form 2276.
2. The subcontractor NCR is not a vehicle for granting variances from LANL institutional documents. If a
nonconformance disposition requires a variance from an institutional document, then the variance
process of the appropriate institutional document (e.g., Engineering Standards) must be followed. See
Table 1.
3. Prior to approving a nonconformance disposition, LANL will consider whether the disposition will
adversely affect important properties of functions of the item or the service.
4. NCRs and supporting documentation that contain controlled unclassified information must be marked
and controlled in accordance with P204-1, Controlled Unclassified Information. Documents that contain
classified information must be marked and controlled in accordance with P204-2, Classified Matter
Protection and Control Handbook.
GENERAL
1. When there is
no STR, STR functions relating to this form will be performed by the LANL technical point
of contact.
2. For construction projects, reference to this form is typically made in Subcontract Exhibit D and/or Exhibit
H.
3. When this form is used by organizations whose job titles are not mentioned in this document, equivalent
functions may be substituted (e.g., Design Authority for FDAR).
4. Submittal of nonconformances from lower-tier subcontractors shall be through the prime subcontractor.
SECTION 1: SUBCONTRACTOR NOTIFICATION TO LANL AND STR RECEIPT
Section Subcontractor Information
1.1 Enter the subcontractor’s document number by which the subcontractor tracks this NCR under their
own system. If none, enter N/A.
1.2 Enter the date on which the subcontractor submits the NCR to LANL.
1.3 Enter the subcontractor’s organization name, address, point of contact name, telephone number,
and POC e-mail address.

[Page 5]
Form 2276 Subcontractor Nonconformance Report (NCR)
Ref. Doc: P330-6 Nonconformance Control and Reporting            Page 2 of 6 Form 2276 Instructions
Revised: [11/19/2020]
Section Subcontractor Information
1.4 Enter LANL subcontract number, purchase order number, and STR’s name.
1.5 Enter supplier or vendor of the item, part number, and item description, if applicable. If none, enter
N/A.
1.6
Enter the date nonconformance detected. Enter the location of the nonconformance, such as the
technical area (TA) and building number.
Enter the method used to detect the nonconformance (e.g., visual inspection, testing, receipt
inspection, etc.).
1.7 List previously submitted NCRs, including dates for same or similar nonconformances, if
applicable. If none, enter N/A.
1.8 Enter the date STR was notified.
1.9
Describe the nonconforming item. Include reference to specific requirement(s) not met, including
drawing, specification, code/standard and citation, as applicable.
Include the extent of condition for each identified item, quantity affected, and key characteristics
such as serial, lot, batch, heat or other numbers as appropriate.
Attach reproducible-quality documents as necessary.
1.10
Select the proposed disposition:
1. Use-As-Is: The item, though nonconforming, is acceptable for its intended use based on an
evaluation by a subject matter expert (engineer of record/design agency when required).
2. Repair: The item must be restored to a condition such that the capability of the item to function
reliably and safely is unimpaired, even though the item does not conform to the original
requirement.  Repaired items must be reexamined in accordance with applicable procedures
and original or alternate acceptance criteria.
3. Rework: The item must be made to conform to original requirements using approved processes
or procedures. Reworked items must be
